correctness.notes: Theorem 2 / \label{thm:vertex} / finite response-type LP \eqref{eq:vlp}: The theorem and proof repeatedly invoke the finite response-type LP \eqref{eq:vlp}, but no equation labelled eq:vlp is present. The reproduced LP should have variables rho_{t,a}>=0 with constraints sum_a rho_{0,a}=p_0, sum_a rho_{1,a}=p_1, sum_a rho_{0,a}a_{m,0}=p_0r_{0,m}, sum_a rho_{1,a}a_{m,1}=p_1r_{1,m}, and objective sum_{t,a}rho_{t,a} ell_a(P), ell_a(P)=sum_m(q_{m|1}a_{m,1}-q_{m|0}a_{m,0}); this object is missing from the note.

perItemFindings.label: vertex
perItemFindings.one_line: [serious] Theorem 2 / \label{thm:vertex} / finite response-type LP \eqref{eq:vlp}: The theorem and proof repeatedly invoke the finite response-type LP \eqref{eq:vlp}, but no equation labelled eq:vlp is present. The reproduced LP should have variables rho_{t,a}>=0 with constraints sum_a rho_{0,a}=p_0, sum_a rho_{1,a}=p_1, sum_a rho_{0,a}a_{m,0}=p_0r_{0,m}, sum_a rho_{1,a}a_{m,1}=p_1r_{1,m}, and objective sum_{t,a}rho_{t,a} ell_a(P), ell_a(P)=sum_m(q_{m|1}a_{m,1}-q_{m|0}a_{m,0}); this object is missing from the note. → The finite-basis theorem is not mathematically well-typed/evaluable as written, and downstream references to basis matrices A_B(lambda), b(P), c_B(P,lambda), and the inference branch maps lack their defining LP source. Adding the displayed LP definition should rescue the claim; no dropped ACE term was found in the intended reduction.

verbatim_critique:
D0.5.1 (math referee) found load-bearing correctness defects — re-derive the flagged step(s):
[serious, conjecture:vertex] Theorem 2 / \label{thm:vertex} / finite response-type LP \eqref{eq:vlp}: The theorem and proof repeatedly invoke the finite response-type LP \eqref{eq:vlp}, but no equation labelled eq:vlp is present. The reproduced LP should have variables rho_{t,a}>=0 with constraints sum_a rho_{0,a}=p_0, sum_a rho_{1,a}=p_1, sum_a rho_{0,a}a_{m,0}=p_0r_{0,m}, sum_a rho_{1,a}a_{m,1}=p_1r_{1,m}, and objective sum_{t,a}rho_{t,a} ell_a(P), ell_a(P)=sum_m(q_{m|1}a_{m,1}-q_{m|0}a_{m,0}); this object is missing from the note.. Fix: The finite-basis theorem is not mathematically well-typed/evaluable as written, and downstream references to basis matrices A_B(lambda), b(P), c_B(P,lambda), and the inference branch maps lack their defining LP source. Adding the displayed LP definition should rescue the claim; no dropped ACE term was found in the intended reduction.

Referee notes: I reproduced the load-bearing algebra for the nondecomposition refutation, the finite response-type endpoint reduction, the active-branch inference limit, and the cross-theorem dependency from vertex geometry to inference. The mathematical term lists match, but Theorem 2's central LP object is referenced as \eqref{eq:vlp} without any displayed definition, so the solve target is not evaluable as written.
